info.title: Aska API
info.summary: Backend API for Aska's visual workspace and archive.
info.description:
Aska is a multi-tenant visual workspace. Workspaces contain collections, folders, and assets such as images and notes. Assets can be organised within collection folder trees or live in the inbox (unplaced).

All endpoints under `/api/v1` require authentication via `authMiddleware`. The authenticated session must belong to the workspace's organisation.

tags.name: Collections
tags.description: Collection CRUD, spatial canvas positions, folder navigation, and node (folder/asset) deletion.

tags.name: Assets
tags.description: Inbox contents, note creation, asset placement and permanent deletion.

tags.name: Image Uploads
tags.description: Direct upload (presigned URL) and remote URL ingestion for images, scoped to a collection or the inbox.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's session, user object, and the ID of the organisation they are currently acting within.

get.summary: Get workspace and light collection list
get.description: Returns workspace metadata together with a lightweight list of its collections (id, name, slug, asset count).
Returns detailed collection records including descriptions, timestamps, asset counts, and preview thumbnails.

post.summary: Create a folder in a collection
post.description: Creates a new folder at the root of the collection or within a parent folder specified by `parentFolderPath`.

post.summary: Create a note in a collection
post.description: Creates a text note asset directly inside a collection, optionally nested within a folder subtree via `parentFolderPath`.

get.summary: Get collection contents at a folder path
get.description: Returns the breadcrumb trail and child nodes (folders, images, notes) at the specified folder path within a collection. Omit `folderPath` to list the collection root.

parameters.name: folderPath
parameters.description: Slash-separated folder path relative to the collection root, e.g. `designs/2024`.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Comma-separated node types to include. Omit to include folders, images, and notes.

200.description: The collection contents at the requested path.

delete.summary: Delete a folder from a collection
delete.description: Recursively deletes the folder, all descendant folders, and all descendant assets (including their S3 storage objects). For deleting individual assets use `DELETE /workspace/{workspaceSlug}/assets/{assetId}`.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Node identifier in the form `{kind}-{id}`, e.g. `folder-42` or `image-7`.

patch.summary: Update collection node canvas positions in one transaction
patch.description: Atomically persists the positions of a dragged group after verifying every node is still in the expected parent folder.

patch.summary: Update a collection node's canvas position
patch.description: Persists the signed integer canvas coordinate for an image, note, or folder after placement or dragging. The request includes the current parent folder ID so a delayed source-canvas position write is rejected after an asset moves.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Node identifier in the form `{kind}-{id}`, e.g. `folder-42` or `image-7`.

patch.summary: Move a collection node into a folder
patch.description: Moves a persisted image, note, or folder from the current collection or folder canvas into a folder in the same collection. The operation is idempotent for a repeated drop onto the same target and resets the moved node's destination position to null. Folder moves preserve descendant placements and positions while transactionally updating cached subtree paths.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Persisted image, note, or folder node identifier, e.g. `image-7`, `note-42`, or `folder-3`.

200.description: The node was moved, or was already in the requested folder.

get.description: Returns all assets (images and notes) that are not placed in any collection. This is the default landing view for orphaned assets.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Comma-separated asset types to include. Inbox has images and notes only.

post.description: Finds images whose extracted palette contains every requested OKLab color. The first increment supports the Inbox and a direct collection folder board; results are ranked by perceptual distance and palette prominence.

200.description: Ranked image results that clear the adaptive relevance cutoff.

post.summary: Create a note in the inbox
post.description: Creates a text note asset in the inbox (unplaced). The note can later be moved to a collection via the placement endpoint.

201.description: The note was created in the inbox.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes the asset, its metadata, and its associated S3 storage objects (original image, display variant, preview variant, and upload object). This action cannot be undone.

To only remove an asset from a collection without deleting it, use `DELETE /collections/{slug}/nodes/{nodeId}` instead.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: Asset identifier in the form `{kind}-{id}`, e.g. `image-42` or `note-17`.

post.summary: Place an inbox asset into a collection
post.description: Moves an asset from the inbox into a collection at the specified folder path. If the asset already belongs to a collection, it is moved (only one placement is allowed per asset).

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: Asset identifier in the form `{kind}-{id}`, e.g. `image-42`.

201.description: The asset was placed in the collection.

post.summary: Initiate a direct image upload to a collection
post.description: Generates a presigned PUT URL for uploading an image file directly to S3 object storage scoped to a collection. S3 creation events start asynchronous processing; poll the upload status endpoint after the PUT succeeds.

201.description: Upload session created. Upload the file to the returned `url` via PUT with the returned `headers`, then poll the upload status endpoint.

200.description: Current asynchronous upload status. Includes the image node after completion.

post.summary: Import an image from a remote URL into a collection
post.description: Downloads an image from a remote URL into S3 and returns an asynchronous upload status. Independent workers generate variants and palette data for the pre-created image asset.

202.description: The remote image was accepted for asynchronous processing.

post.summary: Initiate a direct image upload to the inbox
post.description: Same as `createDirectImageUpload` but scoped to the inbox. S3 creation events start asynchronous processing, and the pre-created asset is enriched as processing completes.

200.description: Current asynchronous upload status. Includes the image node after completion.

post.summary: Import an image from a remote URL into the inbox

202.description: The remote image was accepted for asynchronous processing.

WorkspaceSlug.name: workspaceSlug
WorkspaceSlug.description: The URL slug of the workspace.

CollectionSlug.name: collectionSlug
CollectionSlug.description: The URL slug of the collection within the workspace.

BadRequest.description: The request body or parameters are invalid.

Unauthorized.description: The request does not have a valid session.

Forbidden.description: The authenticated user does not have access to this resource.

NotFound.description: The requested resource (workspace, collection, folder, asset, or upload) was not found.

Conflict.description: The request conflicts with the current state (e.g. duplicate name).

BoardPosition.description: A signed whole-pixel coordinate in collection canvas space.

NullableBoardPosition.description: An authored canvas position, or null for a legacy node using deterministic client fallback placement.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error body returned on all 4xx and 5xx responses.

activeOrganizationId.description: The organisation ID the user is currently acting within.

WorkspaceInfo.description: A workspace (organisation) within the Aska system.

assetCount.description: Total number of assets across all folders in this collection.

assetId.description: Asset node ID used to reconcile optimistic previews.

url.description: Presigned thumbnail URL for image previews.

blurDataURL.description: Blur placeholder data URL for image previews.

FolderChildPreview.description: Preview thumbnail for a folder child.

name.description: Display name for the new collection.
Display name for the new folder.

parentFolderPath.description: Slash-separated path to the parent folder (e.g. `designs/2024`). Omit or leave empty to create at the collection root.

position.description: Optional initial position in the target canvas.

path.description: Full path of the new folder, e.g. `designs/2024/logos`.

count.description: Number of descendant assets. Newly created folders have a count of zero.

color.description: Accent colour for the note (hex or named colour).

parentFolderPath.description: Folder path to create the note in. Omit for collection root.

position.description: Optional initial position in the target canvas.

count.description: Number of descendant assets, including assets in nested folders.

url.description: Presigned URL for the display variant.

originalUrl.description: Presigned URL for the original image file.

dominantColors.description: Image palette colours, ordered by dominance.

sizeBytes.description: Display variant file size in bytes.

Breadcrumb.description: A breadcrumb entry for folder navigation.

parentFolderPath.description: Folder path within the collection to place the asset in. Omit for collection root.

deletedNodeId.description: The identifier of the deleted node, e.g. `folder-42`.

deletedAssetCount.description: Number of assets deleted (0 for asset nodes, 1+ for folder deletions).

expectedParentFolderNodeId.description: Parent folder expected when the drag started, or null for the collection root.
Parent folder expected when the drag started, or null for the collection root.

moved.description: False when the asset was already in the target folder.

deletedAssetId.description: The identifier of the permanently deleted asset, e.g. `image-42`.

fileName.description: Original file name (used for S3 key generation only; not retained as the asset title).

sizeBytes.description: File size in bytes. Default maximum is 20 MB.

title.description: Optional display title for the image asset.

alt.description: Optional alt text for the image.

parentFolderPath.description: Folder path to create the asset in once the upload is completed. Omit for collection root.

position.description: Optional position reserved for the finalized image in the target canvas.

id.description: Upload session ID to use when polling asynchronous processing status.

objectKey.description: S3 object key for the uploaded file.

url.description: Presigned PUT URL for uploading the file.

headers.description: Required HTTP headers to include in the PUT request (e.g. Content-Type).

expiresAt.description: Expiration time of the presigned URL.

maxSizeBytes.description: Maximum allowed file size in bytes.

url.description: Remote URL of the image to import.

parentFolderPath.description: Folder path to place the imported asset in. Omit for collection root.

position.description: Optional position reserved for the finalized image in the target canvas.

ColorSearchOklabColor.description: A color represented in the OKLab perceptual color space.

includeDescendants.description: Recursive scope is reserved for a later navigator-aware increment.
